content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine correctness issues in the prompt contract and rendering logic. Amendment fr-13-7cb89c72 targets a misleading diagnostic error. Amendment fr-13-7b70e4e8 identifies a real escaping/validation gap with Markdown heading semantics. Amendment fr-13-4c0192fe targets semantic flattening of nested list structure that changes scope meaning. I agree with the planner positions on all three.

## Votes

- `fr-13-7cb89c72`: Accept
  Confirmed in task_prompt_contract.rs:181-192. When the contract marker IS present at the top (has_top_level_contract_marker returns true) but both Milestone Summary (index 0) and Current Bead Details (index 1) are missing, prompt_uses_contract (lines 116-136) returns false because it requires a section with index <= 1 after the marker. This causes the else-if branch at line 187 to fire, emitting the misleading error 'contract marker must appear before the canonical section block'. The marker IS correctly placed; the issue is that early sections are absent. The validator still correctly reports the missing sections via lines 222-233 and 244-248, so the prompt is properly rejected, but the false marker-placement error is confusing diagnostic noise. Gating the placement error on whether ANY canonical section heading exists after the marker position would be a clean fix that doesn't weaken rejection behavior.
- `fr-13-7b70e4e8`: Accept
  Confirmed the mismatch. indent_section_body (service.rs:732-744) prefixes body lines with exactly two spaces ('  {line}'). Per the CommonMark spec, ATX headings are valid with up to three leading spaces, meaning '  ## Acceptance Criteria' (two-space indented) is still a valid Markdown heading. Meanwhile canonical_section_heading_index (task_prompt_contract.rs:61-66) uses strip_prefix('## ') which only matches headings starting at column 1 — it would NOT detect '  ## Acceptance Criteria'. This means if a bead description or milestone body contains a line like '## Acceptance Criteria' or '## Review Policy', the renderer will emit it as '  ## Acceptance Criteria', the validator won't flag it as an extra canonical heading, but downstream Markdown readers will parse it as a real heading. This is a real drift hole that could cause structural confusion in prompt consumers. The fix should either escape more aggressively (4+ spaces or fenced blocks) or make the validator detect Markdown-valid headings with leading spaces.
- `fr-13-4c0192fe`: Accept
  Confirmed in service.rs:569-595. collect_section_items trims each line (line.trim() at line 569) before passing to strip_markdown_list_marker, which also calls trim_start() at line 518. This means indented sub-bullets like '  - supporting detail' have their indentation stripped and are treated as new top-level list items. A nested list structure like '- primary criterion' / '  - supporting note' gets flattened into two independent acceptance criteria or non-goals, which changes the semantic meaning of the scope boundary. Since these lists define what is in-scope and out-of-scope for bead execution, promoting sub-items to top-level items is semantically incorrect and could cause downstream stages to interpret scope more broadly than intended. The fix should either preserve indentation context or only treat unindented list markers as new top-level items.
